const App = (): JSX.Element => (
  <AuthProvider>
    <Suspense fallback={<LoadingScreen />}>
      <Routes>
        <Route path="/auth" element={<AuthPage />} />
        <Route path="/onboarding/sector" element={<SectorSelectPage />} />
        <Route element={<ProtectedShell />}>
          <Route index element={<Navigate replace to="/radar" />} />
          <Route path="/radar" element={<RadarPage />} />
          <Route path="/prospect/:id" element={<ProspectDetailPage />} />
          <Route path="/pipeline" element={<PipelinePage />} />
          <Route path="/sources" element={<SourcesPage />} />
          <Route path="/statistiques" element={<StatsPage />} />
          <Route path="/reglages" element={<SettingsPage />} />
        </Route>
        <Route path="*" element={<Navigate replace to="/radar" />} />
      </Routes>
    </Suspense>
  </AuthProvider>
);

export default App;

input.description: Run TypeScript type checking on the project

content.text:
TypeScript compilation passes with zero errors. The implementation is complete. Here is a summary of all changes made to `C:/Users/User/Downloads/SaaS X-Final-Rick-Test - Test Copie/src/App.tsx`:

---

## Changes Made

### 1. Updated Imports (lines 1-6)

- **React**: Added `useState`, `useEffect`, `useRef` to the existing import from `'react'`
- **react-router-dom**: Added `useNavigate` to the existing import
- **lucide-react**: Added new import `{ Search }` for the search icon in the palette
- **useAuth**: Added `useAuth` as a named import alongside `AuthProvider` from `'./hooks/useAuth'`

### 2. Added `CommandPalette` Component (lines 31-133)

Defined as a `React.FC` with typed props (`isOpen`, `onClose`, `onNavigate`) placed right after `ProtectedShell` and before the new `AppContent` component. It includes:

- **State**: `query` (search input) and `inputRef` (for auto-focus)
- **Auto-focus effect**: When `isOpen` becomes true, it focuses the input after 100ms and resets the query
- **19 commands** organized into two categories ("Navigation" and "Outils"), each with an `id`, `label`, `icon`, and `category`
- **Filtering**: Real-time case-insensitive filtering on the command labels
- **Grouping**: Commands are grouped by category using `reduce`
- **UI structure**:
  - Full-screen overlay with backdrop blur (`z-[100]`)
  - Dark modal (`bg-[#13131d]`) with rounded corners and shadow
  - Search input with the Lucide `Search` icon and ESC keyboard hint
  - Scrollable results area (max 340px) with grouped commands
  - Footer with keyboard navigation hints and "CitedBy.ai" branding
- **Interactions**: Clicking the overlay closes the palette; clicking a command triggers `onNavigate` and `onClose`

### 3. Created `AppContent` Component (lines 135-177)

This new component was extracted from the original `App` to hold stateful logic (hooks cannot be used in the original `App` since it wraps `AuthProvider`, and `useAuth`/`useNavigate` must be called inside the provider context). It includes:

- `useAuth()` to get the `user` object (for conditionally rendering the palette only when authenticated)
- `useNavigate()` from react-router-dom for navigation
- `isCommandPaletteOpen` state toggled by the keyboard shortcut
- **Keyboard shortcut handler** (`useEffect`): Ctrl+K (or Cmd+K on Mac) toggles the palette; Escape closes it
- `navigateTo` helper that calls `navigate('/' + view)` to route based on command IDs
- The `CommandPalette` is rendered conditionally with `{user && <CommandPalette ... />}` right before `<Routes>`

### 4. Simplified `App` Component (lines 179-183)

The `App` component now simply wraps `<AppContent />` inside `<AuthProvider>`, keeping the component hierarchy clean:

```tsx
const App = (): JSX.Element => (
  <AuthProvider>
    <AppContent />
  </AuthProvider>
);
```

All existing routes and components (`LoadingScreen`, `ProtectedShell`, lazy-loaded pages) remain unchanged. The TypeScript type check passes cleanly with no errors.
